claim: Eleventh MCC measurement. ComposedDetector = ComplexAssemblyDetector (known-complex subunit lookup from bundled complex_formation.xlsx) OR AnnotationClassDetector (bacterial essential-class keyword rules on syn3a_gene_table.csv product strings) OR PerRuleDetector (trajectory-based catalysis-silencing). All three signals are bundled offline data; zero API / network cost per run. Result: MCC = 0.700 on standard balanced n=40 panel at seed=42, first single-run measurement above the 0.59 brief target.
source_detail: Reproducible via 'python scripts/run_sweep_parallel.py --max-genes 40 --balanced --workers 4 --use-rust --scale 0.05 --t-end-s 0.5 --detector composed --seed 42 --panel-seed 42'. Prediction CSV at outputs/predictions_parallel_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1_w4_composed.csv.

false_positives.reason: iMB155 missing acetaldehyde sink / alt DRPA

caveats: The 3 false positives are all carry-over from v5/v9 and are known architectural gaps in the iMB155 metabolic reconstruction (missing alternate pathways for cholesterol, acetaldehyde, PDH lipoylation). They are unaffected by detector design and would only clear with a network fix.

Both new detectors (complex + annotation) are prior-knowledge lookups with no simulation dependence. Their contribution is orthogonal to the trajectory signal, which is why OR-composition works cleanly rather than averaging or AND'ing.
Single-seed result. The structural + annotation priors are deterministic so seed-variance should be low; recommend a 5-seed replicate run to confirm like v5/v9 did.
All data used is bundled in the repo (complex_formation.xlsx, syn3a_gene_table.csv, Breuer 2019 CSV). No network calls, no API keys, no recurring cost per run.
Brief target was MCC > 0.59. v10 exceeds it by 0.11 on this single-seed run.
